# Alexander Russell - AI Engineer & Agentic Systems Architect

> Personal portfolio and professional profile of Alexander Russell, an AI Engineer based in Christchurch, New Zealand.

## About

Alexander Russell is an AI Engineer and Agentic Systems Architect focused on building tools that drive operational speed. He has been automating professionally since late 2021. His approach combines the technical rigour of Computer Science with the business logic of Information Systems to create secure and scalable automation.

He specialises in agentic AI systems, Model Context Protocol (MCP) integrations, RAG pipelines, and secure full-stack development. From architecting secure, multi-tenant backends for Harkness AI to delivering a 71% productivity gain through automation at IJG Trails, he has a track record of identifying bottlenecks and deploying code to fix them.

- Location: Christchurch, New Zealand
- Email: me@alexrussell.io
- LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/alexrussell-tech
- Website: https://alexrussell.io/

## Current Roles

### AI Engineer (Contract), Harkness AI (Aug 2025 to Present)
Leading the technical architecture and delivery of agentic AI workflows and secure cloud infrastructure for business clients.
- Secure Multi-App Backend: a schema-isolated multi-tenant Supabase backend powering three production applications, with row-level security and strict per-app data isolation
- Multimodal Application: transcribes raw audio and uses the transcript to generate tailored, high-fidelity image assets
- Custom MCP Server: a Model Context Protocol server exposing a payroll system to AI agents, with server-side redaction of sensitive pay, bank, and IRD fields
- Heritage Preservation Platform: frontend and backend work on a culturally sensitive knowledge-preservation platform, focused on data sovereignty and secure authentication

### Founder & Product Engineer, StudyPoints (Dec 2024 to Present)
- Live full-stack SaaS built with React and Firebase
- Automated AI pipeline that synthesises lecture audio into study notes

### Founder & Principal Engineer, Atvora (2026 to Present)
Atvora (https://atvora.com) is a software studio in Christchurch, New Zealand that builds production grade, white label web applications for agencies, shipped in days with a written Evidence Pack documenting how every build was tested and verified.

## Previous Experience

### Process Automation Lead, IJG Trails (Dec 2021 to May 2023)
- Achieved 71% efficiency gain (7 hours reduced to under 2 hours daily)
- Built VBA automation suite for data cleaning and Outlook email dispatch
- Saved approximately 25 hours per week

### Residential Advisor, UniLodge, University of Canterbury (Jan 2024 to Dec 2024)
- Pastoral care for 30 first-year students
- Crisis management and emergency protocols

## Technical Skills

### AI & Agentic
Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers, Agentic workflows, OpenAI / Claude / Gemini, RAG pipelines, MS Copilot Studio, Prompt Engineering

### Full Stack
React.js / Next.js, TypeScript, Supabase (RLS/Auth), Firebase, SQL / Postgres, Vercel

### Automation & Ops
Python, n8n workflows, VBA / Macros, Git / GitHub, Linux

## Education

### B.Sc Computer Science and Information Systems, University of Canterbury (Jul 2023 to Jun 2026)
- A-Grade average with 50% course overload
- Selected for Deloitte x ServiceNow AI Hackathon (2025)
- Key grades: Artificial Intelligence (A+), Web Architectures (A+), Relational Databases (A), Business Intelligence (A)

### National Senior Certificate, Windhoek Afrikaans Private School (Dec 2021)
- 7 Distinctions (Straight A's)
- Computer Applications Technology: 90% (School Record)

- Prompt Engineering for MS Copilot, Microsoft (2025)
- CS50's Introduction to AI with Python, Harvard University (2025)
- Vector Search and Embeddings, Google Cloud (2025)
- AI for Business Leaders, Microsoft (2026)

## Leadership & Community

### AI Forum New Zealand, LLM Working Group (Contributor)
Selected contributor advancing national standards for Agentic AI and Data Sovereignty in Aotearoa.
https://aiforum.org.nz/gen-ai-working-group/

### President, African Society (AfroSoc), University of Canterbury (Jun 2025 to Jun 2026)
Lead executive team in strategic planning, budgeting, and club collaborations.

### International Student Ambassador, University of Canterbury (Jun 2025 to Jun 2026)
University representative managing logistics and cultural integration for global students.
